purpose: Pin the HERO vocabulary frozensets that future ML-layer trainers (C10) will consume as categorical feature inputs.

sha_method.scope: module-level AnnAssign or Assign nodes whose target name starts with HERO_ and ends with _VOCAB
sha_method.note: Both typed (AnnAssign) and untyped (Assign) bindings are hashed so a drive-by removal of the type annotation cannot silently bypass the pin.

drift_policy.cosmetic_docstring: Update SHA only; no schema_version bump. Add a one-line CHANGELOG note.
drift_policy.new_vocab_value_added: MAJOR — bump schema_version to v2 and add ADR (training-data implication).
drift_policy.vocab_value_removed_or_renamed: MAJOR — bump schema_version to v2 and add migration ADR.
drift_policy.new_vocab_set_added: MINOR — append to source_symbols and update SHA; no schema_version bump.

vocabularies.HERO_RISK_VOCAB_note: Empty string is a Pine-gated sentinel meaning 'no dominant risk' (gated by ``!= ""`` on the consumer side).

consumer_contract.categorical_encoding: These vocabularies are the authoritative categorical-feature set for the future ML layer. Encoders must use the exact string values above.
consumer_contract.drift_changes_break: Any vocabulary drift bumps schema_version to v2 and requires a CHANGELOG + library_field_version bump per ADR-0006.

audit_trail.companion_test: tests/test_hero_observed_vocab_pin.py (already enforces vocab-drift detection at the source)
